title: Complex<Q>: arithmetic
analysis: Task 286 established the Complex type infrastructure: Value::Complex { re, im, dimension } in reify-types, and stdlib functions (complex(), re(), im(), conjugate(), phase(), magnitude(), complex_add(), complex_mul()). This task promotes Complex arithmetic from function-call-only (stdlib) to first-class operator support in the expression evaluator (reify-expr), and adds method-call syntax (.re, .im, .magnitude(), .phase(), .conjugate()). The evaluator's eval_add/eval_sub/eval_mul/eval_div/eval_unop functions need Complex arms following the existing Scalar pattern. eval_method_call needs Complex-specific method dispatch following the .x/.y/.z Tensor pattern. Key dimension rules: add/sub require matching dimensions, mul combines dimensions via DimensionVector::mul(), div combines via DimensionVector::div(). The evaluator does NOT sanitize NaN/Inf (consistent with existing Scalar arithmetic); that responsibility stays in stdlib. Complex/Scalar mixed arithmetic uses the scalar's value as a real multiplier/divisor and combines dimensions. Complex/Int and Complex/Real use the numeric as a dimensionless multiplier preserving the Complex's dimension.

prerequisites.description: Create test file crates/reify-expr/tests/complex_eval_tests.rs with imports (reify_expr, reify_types) and helper functions: lit(v, ty) wrapping CompiledExpr::literal, complex_val(re, im, dim) building Value::Complex, and scalar_val(v, dim) building Value::Scalar — matching existing test patterns from point_vector_eval_tests.rs and the inline tests module

steps.description: Write failing tests for Complex + Complex: (1) complex_add_same_dimension — two Complex<Length> values added return Complex with summed re/im and preserved dimension; (2) complex_add_dimension_mismatch — Complex<Length> + Complex<Time> returns Undef

Add Value::Complex arm to eval_add in lib.rs: match (Complex{re:ar,im:ai,dimension:ad}, Complex{re:br,im:bi,dimension:bd}), check ad==bd (Undef if not), return Complex{re:ar+br, im:ai+bi, dimension:*ad}. Insert before the _ => Value::Undef catch-all
Write failing tests for Complex - Complex: (1) complex_sub_same_dimension — subtraction returns Complex with differenced re/im and preserved dimension; (2) complex_sub_dimension_mismatch → Undef
Add Value::Complex arm to eval_sub in lib.rs: same pattern as add but with subtraction. Check dimension match, return Complex{re:ar-br, im:ai-bi, dimension:*ad}

Write failing tests for Complex * Complex: (1) complex_mul_dimensionless — (1+2i)*(3+4i) = (-5+10i) with DIMENSIONLESS; (2) complex_mul_dimension_product — Complex<Length> * Complex<Time> returns Complex with LENGTH.mul(TIME) dimension, verifying (ac-bd)+(ad+bc)i formula
Add Complex×Complex arm to eval_mul: compute re=ar*br-ai*bi, im=ar*bi+ai*br, dimension=ad.mul(bd). Return Value::Complex{re, im, dimension}

Write failing tests for Complex * Scalar and Scalar * Complex: (1) complex_mul_scalar_right — Complex<Length> * Scalar<Time> scales re/im and combines dimensions; (2) scalar_mul_complex_left — Scalar<Time> * Complex<Length> is commutative with same result; (3) complex_mul_int and int_mul_complex — dimensionless integer multiplier preserves Complex dimension; (4) complex_mul_real and real_mul_complex — same for Real

Add mixed multiplication arms to eval_mul: (Complex,Scalar)|(Scalar,Complex) — multiply re/im by si_value, combine dimensions via mul(); (Complex,Int)|(Int,Complex) — multiply re/im by n as f64, preserve Complex dimension; (Complex,Real)|(Real,Complex) — multiply re/im by r, preserve Complex dimension. Place before the _ catch-all

Write failing tests for Complex division: (1) complex_div_scalar — Complex<Area>/Scalar<Length> returns Complex<Length> with re/im divided by si_value; (2) complex_div_int — Complex / 2 halves re/im, preserves dimension; (3) complex_div_real — Complex / 2.0 same; (4) complex_div_by_zero_int → Undef; (5) complex_div_by_zero_scalar → Undef

Add Complex division arms to eval_div: (Complex,Scalar) — divide re/im by si_value, combine dimensions via div(); (Complex,Int) — divide by n as f64, preserve dimension; (Complex,Real) — divide by r, preserve dimension. Division-by-zero already handled by the existing guard at top of eval_div
Write failing test for unary -Complex: complex_negation — negating Complex<Length>(3.0, 4.0) returns Complex<Length>(-3.0, -4.0) with same dimension
Add Value::Complex arm to eval_unop UnOp::Neg: return Value::Complex { re: -re, im: -im, dimension }

Write failing tests for .re and .im methods: (1) method_re_dimensionless — Complex(3.0,4.0,DIMENSIONLESS).re returns Real(3.0); (2) method_re_dimensioned — Complex(3.0,4.0,LENGTH).re returns Scalar{3.0,LENGTH}; (3) method_im_dimensionless — returns Real(4.0); (4) method_im_dimensioned — returns Scalar{4.0,LENGTH}. Uses CompiledExpr::method_call with zero args

Add 're' and 'im' arms to eval_method_call: match on Value::Complex, check args.is_empty() (Undef if not), extract re or im component. Return Real if dimension.is_dimensionless(), Scalar{si_value, dimension} otherwise. Insert before the _ catch-all

Write failing tests for .magnitude(): (1) method_magnitude_dimensionless — Complex(3.0,4.0,DIMENSIONLESS).magnitude() returns Real(5.0); (2) method_magnitude_dimensioned — Complex(3.0,4.0,LENGTH).magnitude() returns Scalar{5.0,LENGTH}
Add 'magnitude' arm to eval_method_call for Value::Complex: compute (re*re + im*im).sqrt(), return Real if dimensionless, Scalar with preserved dimension otherwise. Check args.is_empty()
Write failing test for .phase(): method_phase — Complex(1.0, 1.0, LENGTH).phase() returns Scalar with ANGLE dimension and value PI/4 (≈0.7854). Verify dimension is ANGLE regardless of input dimension
Add 'phase' arm to eval_method_call for Value::Complex: compute im.atan2(re), return Scalar{si_value: angle, dimension: DimensionVector::ANGLE}. Check args.is_empty()
Write failing test for .conjugate(): method_conjugate — Complex(3.0, 4.0, LENGTH).conjugate() returns Complex(3.0, -4.0, LENGTH)
Add 'conjugate' arm to eval_method_call for Value::Complex: return Complex{re: *re, im: -im, dimension: *dimension}. Check args.is_empty()

Write edge-case tests: (1) complex_add_non_complex_undef — Complex + Int/Real/Scalar returns Undef; (2) method_re_on_non_complex_undef — calling .re on a Scalar returns Undef; (3) method_magnitude_with_args_undef — .magnitude(1) with unexpected args returns Undef; (4) complex_undef_propagation — Undef + Complex returns Undef (already handled by eval_binop, but verify)

Verify all edge-case tests pass — they should be covered by the existing _ => Value::Undef catch-all arms in eval_add/eval_method_call and Undef propagation in eval_binop. Fix any failures if catch-all ordering is wrong

design_decisions.decision: No NaN/Inf sanitization in evaluator for Complex arithmetic
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with existing Scalar arithmetic in eval_mul/eval_div/eval_add which do NOT call sanitize_value. Sanitization is the stdlib's responsibility (defense-in-depth at the function call boundary). The evaluator is a pure arithmetic engine.

design_decisions.decision: Complex/Int and Complex/Real treat the numeric as a dimensionless scalar multiplier
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the established pattern from Scalar*Int and Scalar*Real in eval_mul: the numeric preserves the Complex's dimension. This is the natural mathematical interpretation.

design_decisions.decision: Method names match stdlib function names: 're', 'im', 'magnitude', 'phase', 'conjugate'
design_decisions.rationale: The stdlib already defines these as function names (re(), im(), etc.). Using identical names for methods ensures consistency: z.re and re(z) are semantically equivalent.

design_decisions.decision: Methods .re and .im are zero-arg method calls (not field access)
design_decisions.rationale: The evaluator dispatch is via MethodCall { object, method, args } — there is no FieldAccess expression kind. The task describes .re and .im as accessors, so we implement them as zero-argument method calls, matching the .x/.y/.z pattern for Tensor components.

design_decisions.decision: Complex division only supports Complex/Scalar (not Complex/Complex)
design_decisions.rationale: The task description explicitly lists Complex/Scalar but not Complex/Complex division. Complex/Complex division (via conjugate multiplication) is a separate concern and not requested.

design_decisions.decision: All tests in a separate integration test file
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the established pattern: complex topics get their own test file in crates/reify-expr/tests/ (see point_vector_eval_tests.rs, collection_eval_tests.rs, lambda_eval_tests.rs).

design_decisions.decision: .magnitude() returns Real for dimensionless Complex, Scalar for dimensioned
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the stdlib magnitude() function behavior and the re()/im() extraction pattern. A dimensionless Complex's magnitude is a plain real number.

reuse.how: Add Complex match arms following the exact same pattern: dimension check for add/sub, dimension.mul()/div() for mul/div
Add Complex arm with simple negation of both re and im, much simpler than Tensor's component-wise pattern
Add 're'/'im' as zero-arg methods extracting Complex components, following same match-on-method-name pattern
Use for Complex*Complex and Complex*Scalar dimension algebra, identical to Scalar*Scalar usage

reuse.what: Test helpers: lit(), mm_val() pattern from inline tests
reuse.how: Replicate in test file with additional complex_val() helper that builds Value::Complex
Same mathematical formulas for Complex addition (re+re, im+im) and multiplication ((ac-bd)+(ad+bc)i) are reused in the evaluator arms
Use in tests to build MethodCall expressions for .re, .im, .magnitude(), .phase(), .conjugate()
